executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: The architecture watchdog verifier previously allowed stale independent signoff to pass even after newer runtime evidence existed, leaving a hidden self-certification gap.
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Require the architecture independent-verification artifact to be newer than the latest relevant runtime evidence before the verifier can pass.

audit_metadata.scheduler_inspection_method: direct openclaw cron list --json and live jobs.json comparison

owns: bounded timeout reruns and timeout tuning
artifact contract checks for code-backed shared-market reuse
syncing research artifacts into the git repo

findings.title: Architecture verifier previously failed open on stale independent verification
findings.mechanism: agent_architecture_verifier.py only checked whether the independent verification artifact existed, was fresh-ish, and said pass; it did not require that signoff to postdate newer runtime evidence such as loop integrity or health-monitor outputs.
findings.root_cause: The watchdog verifier enforced presence but not coherence of independent verification against the latest runtime state.
findings.recommended_fix: Fail closed whenever the independent verification artifact predates newer architecture/runtime evidence and require a fresh independent verification rerun after any newer audit or repair output.

findings.title: Marketing owner loop is learning explicitly, but measurable repo adoption remains flat
findings.mechanism: The marketing audit shows a live self-improvement mandate, owned repair actions, and fresh independent verification, but Codeberg adoption deltas remain flat in the current window.
findings.root_cause: Outcome movement has not yet caught up with the repaired marketing loop design and its new tactic replacement window.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep the marketing loop in owned repair/measurement mode and require new structural marketing capabilities or tactic replacement if Codeberg deltas stay flat through the next window.

findings.title: Retired jobs remain present only as explicitly disabled legacy topology
findings.mechanism: Three disabled jobs remain in jobs.json for audit history but are clearly non-live.
findings.root_cause: Legacy retention is explicit rather than silently authoritative.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep disabled legacy jobs explicitly labeled and non-authoritative.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Keep architecture independent verification freshness-gated against newer runtime evidence
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: This closes the watchdog’s own hidden self-certification hole.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: The architecture path cannot claim healthy status on a stale independent signoff after newer repairs or evidence appear.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Keep marketing learning outcome-focused until Codeberg adoption moves
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: The system now has explicit learning repairs, but measurable progress is still pending.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: The marketing loop remains accountable for outcome movement instead of reporting activity as success.

repairs_applied_this_run.change: Added freshness coherence checks so independent verification must postdate newer architecture/runtime evidence, including loop integrity, health monitor, docs verifier, and shared market-intelligence artifacts.
Added a dedicated machine-readable independent verification pass for the architecture watchdog so fresh signoff can be regenerated from live evidence instead of hand-maintained state.

independent_verification.summary: Independent verification confirms the repaired architecture verifier now fails closed on stale signoff, the live loop topology/ownership checks remain green, and shared market-intelligence reuse stays machine-verifiable.

highest_risk_unresolved_issue.title: Marketing outcome movement is still unproven in the current measurement window
highest_risk_unresolved_issue.why: The owner loop is structurally healthier and independently verified, but Codeberg adoption remains flat, so measurable progress is not yet demonstrated.
